clause_text:
LinkedIn User Agreement
Effective on November 3, 2025
annotator_notes: This is only the agreement title and effective date, so it does not create a substantive privacy, content, liability, or dispute-resolution risk.

clause_text:
Content License to LinkedIn
You own all of your original content that you provide to us, but you also grant us a non-exclusive license to it.
annotator_notes: This clause is content_rights because the user must license their content to LinkedIn in order to use the service. The risk is medium because LinkedIn gets reuse rights over user content even though ownership formally stays with the user.
matched_keywords: grant us a license, you grant a license, non-exclusive license

clause_text: You grant LinkedIn and our Affiliates the following non-exclusive license to the content and other information you provide to our Services: A worldwide, transferable and sublicensable right to use, copy, modify, distribute, publicly perform and display, host, and process your content and other information without any further consent, notice and/or compensation to you or others.
annotator_notes: This clause is content_rights because it gives LinkedIn and its affiliates a very broad worldwide, transferable, sublicensable license to use, modify, distribute, and display user content. The risk is high because the rights are extensive and can be exercised without further consent, notice, or compensation.

clause_text: You can end this license for specific content by deleting such content from the Services, or generally by closing your account, except to the extent you shared it with others as part of the Services and they copied, re-shared it or stored it, we had already sublicensed others prior to your content removal or closing of your account, or we are required by law to retain or share it with others.
annotator_notes: This clause is content_rights because it explains that LinkedIn's license can survive deletion in several cases, such as prior sharing, sublicensing, or legal obligations. The risk is medium because users may not fully regain control over content even after removing it or closing the account.

clause_text: By submitting suggestions or other feedback regarding our Services to LinkedIn, you agree that LinkedIn can use and share such feedback for any purpose without compensation to you.
annotator_notes: This clause is content_rights because it lets LinkedIn freely use and share submitted feedback for any purpose. The risk is medium because users give up control over ideas or suggestions without any compensation.

clause_text:
Recommendations and Automated Processing
We use data and other information about you to make and order relevant suggestions and to generate content for you and others. We use the data and other information that you provide and that we have about Members and content on the Services to make recommendations for connections, content, ads, and features that may be useful to you. For example, that data and information may be used to recommend jobs to you and you to recruiters and to organize content in your feed.
annotator_notes: This clause is tracking_profiling because LinkedIn uses user data to generate recommendations, rank content, and target ads or job suggestions. The risk is medium because it relies on behavioral and profile-based inference to shape what users and recruiters see.

clause_text:
Disclaimer and Limit of Liability
LINKEDIN AND ITS AFFILIATES MAKE NO REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY ABOUT THE SERVICES, INCLUDING ANY REPRESENTATION THAT THE SERVICES WILL BE UNINTERRUPTED OR ERROR-FREE, AND PROVIDE THE SERVICES ON AN "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE" BASIS. TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED UNDER APPLICABLE LAW, LINKEDIN AND ITS AFFILIATES DISCLAIM ANY IMPLIED OR STATUTORY WARRANTY, INCLUDING ANY IMPLIED WARRANTY OF TITLE, ACCURACY, NON-INFRINGEMENT, MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
annotator_notes: This clause is liability_limitation because LinkedIn broadly disclaims warranties and provides the service on an 'as is' and 'as available' basis. The risk is high because it significantly reduces the company's responsibility if the service is inaccurate, unavailable, or harmful.
matched_keywords: fullest extent permitted, to the fullest extent

clause_text: TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, LINKEDIN AND ITS AFFILIATES, WILL NOT BE LIABLE IN CONNECTION WITH THIS CONTRACT FOR LOST PROFITS OR LOST BUSINESS OPPORTUNITIES, REPUTATION, LOSS OF DATA OR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, SPECIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES.
annotator_notes: This clause is liability_limitation because LinkedIn excludes liability for lost profits, data loss, reputational harm, and other indirect or consequential damages. The risk is high because it sharply limits the user's ability to recover for serious harms.
matched_keywords: will not be liable for, fullest extent permitted, to the fullest extent

clause_text: LINKEDIN AND ITS AFFILIATES WILL NOT BE LIABLE TO YOU IN CONNECTION WITH THIS CONTRACT FOR ANY AMOUNT THAT EXCEEDS (A) THE TOTAL FEES PAID OR PAYABLE BY YOU TO LINKEDIN FOR THE SERVICES DURING THE TERM OF THIS CONTRACT, IF ANY, OR (B) US $1000.
annotator_notes: This clause is liability_limitation because it caps LinkedIn's financial exposure to fees paid or US $1000. The risk is high because the cap may be far below the actual harm suffered by a user.

clause_text:
Termination
Both you and LinkedIn may terminate this Contract at any time with notice to the other. On termination, you lose the right to access or use the Services. LinkedIn may assign this Contract to its affiliates or a party that buys it without your consent.
annotator_notes: This clause mainly covers termination rights and contract assignment, which do not fit the project's risk categories closely enough to label here.

clause_text:
Payment Storage
We may store and continue billing your payment method, even after it has expired, to avoid interruptions in your paid Services and to use it to pay for other Services you may buy. If your primary payment method fails, we may automatically charge a secondary payment method, if you have provided one.
annotator_notes: This clause is data_retention because LinkedIn states it may store payment methods and continue billing even after expiration to avoid service interruption. The risk is medium because retained billing data can continue to be used for charges beyond the immediate transaction.

clause_text:
Service Availability
We may change, suspend or discontinue any of our Services. We may also limit the availability of features, content and other information so that they are not available to all Visitors or Members.
annotator_notes: This clause concerns service changes and availability limits, which are important contract terms but do not directly match the project's annotation categories.

clause_text: LinkedIn is not a storage service. You agree that we have no obligation to store, maintain or provide you a copy of any content or other information that you or others provide, except to the extent required by applicable law.
annotator_notes: This clause says LinkedIn is not obligated to store user content, which is a service limitation rather than a retention practice that creates a privacy or access risk under the defined labels.

clause_text:
Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
For others outside of Designated Countries: You and LinkedIn agree that the laws of the State of California, U.S.A., excluding its conflict of laws rules, shall exclusively govern any dispute relating to this Contract and the Services. You and LinkedIn both agree that all claims and disputes can be litigated only in the federal or state courts in Santa Clara County, California, USA, and you and LinkedIn each agree to personal jurisdiction in those courts.
annotator_notes: This clause is labeled arbitration because it restricts how and where disputes can be brought by requiring litigation in specified California courts. The risk is medium because it limits user flexibility and can make legal claims harder to pursue, even though it is forum selection rather than classic arbitration.

clause_text:
Professional Data and Public Information
When you use our Services, you share professional information that others can see, copy and use. Our Services allow sharing of information in many ways, such as through your profile, posts, articles, group posts, links to news articles, job postings, messages, and InMails. Depending on the feature and choices you make, information that you share may be seen by other Members, Visitors, or others on or off of the Services.
annotator_notes: This clause is data_sharing because it explains that professional information users post can be seen, copied, and used by others both on and off the service. The risk is high because shared information can spread beyond LinkedIn and may be difficult for the user to control once disclosed.

clause_text: We also use the data and other information that you provide and that we have about Members and content on the Services to make recommendations for connections, content, ads, and features that may be useful to you, and to organize content in your feed to optimize your experience.
annotator_notes: This clause is tracking_profiling because LinkedIn uses member and content data to personalize recommendations, ads, and feed organization. The risk is medium because the service profiles behavior and preferences to influence user experience.

clause_text:
Data Shared with Affiliates
Affiliates are companies controlling, controlled by or under common control with us, including LinkedIn Ireland, LinkedIn Corporation, LinkedIn Singapore and Microsoft Corporation or any of its subsidiaries such as Github, Inc.
annotator_notes: This clause is data_sharing because it identifies the affiliate entities that may be involved in LinkedIn's internal data flows. The risk is low because it is mostly definitional, but it still signals that user information may circulate across a broader corporate group.
matched_keywords: linkedin ireland, linkedin corporation, linkedin singapore and microsoft corporation, microsoft corporation or any of its subsidiaries

clause_text: We may share information with LinkedIn's affiliates and subsidiaries including Microsoft and Github to carry out our daily business operations and maintain and improve the Services.
annotator_notes: This clause is data_sharing because LinkedIn may share information with affiliates and subsidiaries such as Microsoft and GitHub for operations and service improvement. The risk is medium because user data moves across multiple related entities for broad business purposes.
matched_keywords: including microsoft and github, affiliates and subsidiaries
